verses.text: Wise men from the east come seeking 'he that is born King of the Jews,' having seen His star. Gentiles seek Him. The first worshippers come from afar, drawn by God.
Wise men from the east come seeking 'he that is born King of the Jews,' having seen His star. Gentiles seek Him. The first worshippers come from afar, drawn by God.
Herod is troubled; the scribes point to Bethlehem, as Micah foretold. Knowledge without love. They knew the Scripture yet did not go to worship.
Herod is troubled; the scribes point to Bethlehem, as Micah foretold. Knowledge without love. They knew the Scripture yet did not go to worship.
Herod is troubled; the scribes point to Bethlehem, as Micah foretold. Knowledge without love. They knew the Scripture yet did not go to worship.
Herod is troubled; the scribes point to Bethlehem, as Micah foretold. Knowledge without love. They knew the Scripture yet did not go to worship.
Herod sends the wise men, feigning he too will worship. Hidden malice. The hypocrite cloaks murderous intent in pious words.
Herod sends the wise men, feigning he too will worship. Hidden malice. The hypocrite cloaks murderous intent in pious words.
The star leads them to the child; they fall down and worship, offering gold, frankincense, and myrrh. True homage. They give their best to the new-born King.
The star leads them to the child; they fall down and worship, offering gold, frankincense, and myrrh. True homage. They give their best to the new-born King.
The star leads them to the child; they fall down and worship, offering gold, frankincense, and myrrh. True homage. They give their best to the new-born King.
Warned in a dream, they return another way; an angel bids Joseph flee to Egypt. Divine protection. God guards His Son from the tyrant's reach.
Warned in a dream, they return another way; an angel bids Joseph flee to Egypt. Divine protection. God guards His Son from the tyrant's reach.
Joseph takes the child by night to Egypt, fulfilling, 'Out of Egypt have I called my son.' Refuge abroad. The Saviour shares the path of His people's exile.
Joseph takes the child by night to Egypt, fulfilling, 'Out of Egypt have I called my son.' Refuge abroad. The Saviour shares the path of His people's exile.
Herod slaughters the children of Bethlehem, fulfilling Rachel's weeping. Cruel rage. The world's hatred of Christ strikes first at the innocent.
Herod slaughters the children of Bethlehem, fulfilling Rachel's weeping. Cruel rage. The world's hatred of Christ strikes first at the innocent.
Herod slaughters the children of Bethlehem, fulfilling Rachel's weeping. Cruel rage. The world's hatred of Christ strikes first at the innocent.
After Herod's death, Joseph returns and settles in Nazareth, 'He shall be called a Nazarene.' Home at last. God overrules even a tyrant's death to fulfil His word.
After Herod's death, Joseph returns and settles in Nazareth, 'He shall be called a Nazarene.' Home at last. God overrules even a tyrant's death to fulfil His word.
